reasoning: The target of 10-12 successful Starship launches reaching space in 2025 represents a very specific and relatively narrow slice of potential outcomes. While SpaceX is aggressively ramping up its launch cadence following successful flights in 2024, achieving a near monthly pace (10-12 per year) requires avoiding significant anomalies, regulatory delays (FAA licensing), and production bottlenecks. Based on the historical ramp-up of the Falcon 9 program and the complexities of Starship, getting exactly 10-12 space-reaching flights is possible but competes with the high likelihood of fewer than 10 flights due to typical aerospace delays. Therefore, the probability inherently sits in the 15-25% range for this specific bracket.
